---
name: wonder-govern-check-policy
description: Evaluate project or change compliance against Wonder Govern standards.
---

# Check Policy

Evaluate project or change compliance against `.wonder/standards/`.

## Procedure

scope

- Determine whether the check covers the whole project or a specific change.
- Collect target files, changed files, or diff context when applicable.
- Confirm `.wonder/standards/` exists before checking.

index-standards

- Parse standards files.
- Extract rule ids, titles, severities, and source files.
- Validate rule ids use `GOV-<DOMAIN>-<NUMBER>`.
- Validate severities use critical, high, medium, low, or info.
- Write the applied rule snapshot to `.wonder/runs/<run-id>/standards-index.json`.

inspect

- Gather evidence from files, diffs, validation output, logs, and relevant project context.
- Run read-only validation commands only when useful.
- Record skipped checks and unavailable commands in `.wonder/runs/<run-id>/inspect.md`.

evaluate

- Map evidence to policy violations.
- Assign severity from the violated rule.
- Assign confidence from evidence strength: high, medium, low.
- Use lower confidence when a violation is plausible but uncertain.
- Create remediation text for each violation.

report

- Write `.wonder/runs/<run-id>/violations.json`.
- Write `.wonder/runs/<run-id>/report.md`.
- Update `.wonder/reports/govern-latest.json`.
- Preserve the previous completed run id when a policy check fails.
- If no standards exist, report that standards must be defined first and recommend `wonder-govern.define-standards`.

Do not modify project files under review.
Do not modify `.wonder/standards/`.
Do not define new standards.
Do not run write-mode formatters, auto-fix commands, code generation writes, or dependency installation without explicit consent.
